headline: ADP Jobs Miss: 98K vs Expectations
detail: Softer private payrolls reduce pressure on the Fed to keep rates elevated longer, a net positive for rate-sensitive growth and tech names. For your book - QQQ, SMH, CRWD, SOXL - this is a modest tailwind, but it also raises the stakes on Friday's official NFP report as the real verdict.
rec: No trade needed off this alone - it supports holding your current tech/chip positions, but wait for Friday's official jobs number before adding leverage. If NFP also misses, that's a stronger green light to add to QQQ or SMH.

headline: Nomura: Hyperscaler Spending Will Keep Chip Rally Alive
detail: Nomura specifically flags overlooked bottlenecks and forced hyperscaler capex as the engine keeping chip stocks moving - this directly validates your SMH, SOXL, and CRWD thesis. It's not a new surprise catalyst, but it's institutional backing for staying in your core positions rather than trimming into strength.
rec: This supports holding SMH and SOXL full-size. If SMH pulls back toward the 620-630 zone, that's a level worth adding - but scanner has no SMH setup today, so no immediate entry to chase.

headline: One in Five U.S. Companies Using AI - Job Market Impact Narrow
detail: Goldman data showing AI adoption at 20% of companies with limited job displacement so far is a double-edged read for your names: adoption is real and accelerating (chip demand stays strong) but the 'narrow impact' framing may temper hype-driven multiple expansion short-term. Net effect on your book is small but slightly supportive of the secular demand story.
rec: No trade warranted - this is background confirmation of your existing chip and AI infrastructure thesis, not a new signal. Stay the course on CRWD, SMH, and SOXL.

headline: UK Defense Spending $20B Boost - Defense Stocks Rally
detail: This is a UK/defense story that doesn't directly move your tech and chip names. RTX does appear on the scanner watchlist (7/12, grade A entry 188.59-188.77, stop 187.87) and could benefit indirectly from the defense spending narrative, but it's outside your core 90% tech concentration.
rec: RTX is worth a look if you want sector diversification as a small hedge against tech volatility - scanner entry 188.59-188.77, stop 187.87, but this is a speculative add outside your core strategy, size very small if at all.
